# v2.2.0 — Extended Cloud Resource Discovery

Token calculations now cover 13 additional cloud resource types identified in the Engineering token spreadsheet. These are resources that consume tokens but were previously not scanned.

## New Resource Types

### AWS (5 new)
- **Elastic IPs** — static IP addresses, counted as Active IPs
- **NAT Gateways** — counted as Managed Assets
- **Transit Gateways** — counted as Managed Assets
- **VPN Gateways** — counted as Managed Assets
- **Customer Gateways** — counted as Managed Assets

### Azure (3 new)
- **VPN Gateways** (Virtual Network Gateways) — counted as Managed Assets
- **Azure Firewalls** — counted as Managed Assets
- **Virtual Hubs** (Virtual WAN hubs) — counted as Managed Assets

### GCP (5 new)
- **Compute Addresses** — static/reserved IPs, counted as Active IPs
- **Cloud Routers** — counted as Managed Assets
- **VPN Gateways** — counted as Managed Assets
- **Forwarding Rules** (load balancer frontends) — counted as Managed Assets
- **Internal Ranges** — projected as Address Blocks, counted as DDI Objects

## Notes

- All new resources follow the same partial-failure pattern as existing scans — if one resource type fails, the rest continue
- No new cloud permissions are required beyond the existing read-only roles (these resources use the same `ec2:Describe*`, `compute.readonly`, and `Network Reader` permissions)
- GCP Internal Ranges uses the Network Connectivity API (`networkconnectivity.googleapis.com`), which may need to be enabled in the GCP project

## Download

| Platform | File |
|----------|------|
| Windows | `uddi-token-calculator_windows_amd64.exe` |
| macOS (Apple Silicon) | `uddi-token-calculator_darwin_arm64` |
| Linux | `uddi-token-calculator_linux_amd64` |
